intro: Orobus incurvus, also known as Incurved Grass-pea, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Orobus incurvus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Orobus incurvus is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it may also be used to make tinctures and capsules for the treatment of various ailments.
